sutta: and 40 7 the buddha said monks there are these 4 seasons which if rightly developed rightly revolved gradually bring about the destruction of the asavas what for hearing dhamma in due season discussion of dharma in due season tranquilization samata in due season contemplation vipassana in due season these are the 4 just as monks on the hilltop when the sky deva rains thick drops that water pouring down according to the slope of the ground fills up the clubs chasms and gullies of the hillside when these are filled they fill the pools and these are filled they fill the lakes when these are filled they fill the rivulets and these are filled filled up the great rivers the great rivers being filled filled the sea the ocean just so amongst these 4 seasons if rightly developed rightly revolved gradually bring about the destruction of the asavas so these 4 things are quite important in the spiritual path first 1 is hearing dhamma in the mangala suta this is mentioned dhamma savanna hearing dhamma listening to the dhamma like we are doing now the second 1 is discussion of dhamma discussion of dharma sometimes to clear our doubts we discuss dhamma we shouldn't be afraid to discuss dhamma and he should never get angry in discussing dharma and third 1 is samata tranquilization of the mind fourth 1 is vipassana contemplation so you see in the sutasana the buddha always mention these 2 as necessary conditions not as not 1 by itself samatha and vipassana are these are the 2 things that we do in meditation these are the 2 possible things that we can do in meditation so this is 1 of the sutas that confirms that samatha and vipassana both are necessary and not just 1 now we come to another suta
